whyItMatters: This is about as real a combat validation as autonomy gets right now — not a demo, but 1,100+ missions and lives saved on the Ukrainian front. Worth watching for what a six-month build-to-deploy timeline signals about how fast the Army can field autonomy at scale when it wants to.

title: Kraken Technology Group Raises $175M Series B at $1B Valuation
shortDescription: British maritime autonomy startup Kraken Technology Group raised a $175M Series B at a $1B valuation, led by DTCP with participation from the NATO Innovation Fund, Rheinmetall, and the British Business Bank, to scale production of its uncrewed surface vessels.
whyItMatters: Another European defense unicorn minted, and another sign that NATO's own innovation fund is putting real capital behind allied maritime autonomy. Kraken's partnerships with Anduril and Rheinmetall are also a good template for how a smaller ally-based startup scales through primes rather than around them.

title: Pentagon Awards $86M in OTAs to nLIGHT and Lockheed Martin for Laser Weapons
shortDescription: The Pentagon awarded nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Martin Aculight a combined $86M in Other Transaction Authority agreements (with a program ceiling of $847M) to build 150kW containerized laser weapons for cruise missile and drone defense, under the Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering.
whyItMatters: Directed energy is moving from PowerPoint to procurement — $2B is earmarked for it in the FY27 budget request, and this OTA structure (rapid prototyping, program ceiling nearly 10x the initial award) is the acquisition-reform pattern SVDG members should recognize and expect more of.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B, Backed by Lockheed Martin Ventures
shortDescription: Venus Aerospace raised a $91M Series B led by Mercury Fund, with Lockheed Martin Ventures among the participants, to scale production of its rotating detonation rocket engine (RDRE) for both DoD and commercial space applications.
whyItMatters: Direct sponsor engagement worth flagging to the team: Lockheed Martin Ventures backing a dual-use engine architecture that's explicitly designed to serve one weapons contract and one space contract off the same production line is exactly the kind of capital-plus-customer story SVDG likes to track.

title: NATO Unveils 'Front Door for Industry' and Innovation Scale-Up Package at Ankara Summit
shortDescription: At the NATO Summit Defence Industry Forum in Ankara, NATO announced a single-entry 'Front Door for Industry' for companies to access alliance procurement and innovation opportunities, plus a public unclassified demand signal outlining capability priorities, alongside the new NATO Drone Edge counter-drone initiative.
whyItMatters: This is the alliance-level version of the acquisition-reform push SVDG cares about domestically — a standing complaint from non-traditional vendors has been that there's no single door into NATO procurement. Worth tracking closely for members with allied ambitions.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract for Orbital Laser Tech
shortDescription: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup building satellite-to-satellite laser power beaming and debris-tracking tech, won a $40M Space Force OTA — the largest contract in the company's history — with the government now its largest customer over commercial demand.
whyItMatters: A small dual-use space startup going from a $1.9M Air Force contract to a $40M Space Force award in about two years is a decent proxy for how fast the government is willing to move once a technology proves out — good signal for the capital formation crowd.

title: Pentagon Seeks to Shift $4.3B to Cover Rising Personnel and Operations Costs
shortDescription: The Pentagon submitted a reprogramming notification to Congress to move $4.3B within FY26 accounts, with $3.76B going to military personnel accounts to cover higher end-strength, pay, and PCS costs. The money is being pulled from Army Guard training, Navy R&D and aircraft procurement, Air Force F-35 procurement, and Space Force R&D lines.
whyItMatters: This is the budget-crunch signal to watch — when personnel costs (driven partly by Iran war operations) start cannibalizing weapons R&D and procurement lines, it's a preview of the kind of tradeoffs that could hit program timelines and vendor payment cycles later this year.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
author: Rachel Jewett
shortDescription: CesiumAstro filed with the FCC for Synchronicity, a 737-satellite NGSO constellation providing reconfigurable Ku/Ka/V-band connectivity to fixed and mobile users — the company's first public move toward operating its own constellation rather than just supplying payloads and terminals.
whyItMatters: This is CesiumAstro leveling up from payload supplier to full constellation operator, a real strategic bet from an SVDG sponsor that's already banked $470M in growth capital plus $200M in EXIM financing this year — worth flagging to the group as a name to watch.

title: Helsing Raises $1.8B Series E at $18B Valuation — Europe's Largest Defense-Startup Round
shortDescription: Munich-based Helsing raised $1.8B in a Series E at an $18B valuation — the biggest defense-tech startup round in European history — with demand from Dragoneer, Iconiq, CPPIB, JPMorgan, Lightspeed, and Goldman Sachs Alternatives well exceeding the allocation.
whyItMatters: This is the clearest signal yet of how much institutional capital is chasing a European defense champion — Helsing is now among the continent's most valuable defense companies just four years in. Worth watching as a benchmark for capital formation and valuations on this side of the Atlantic.

title: Auterion and Skyfall to Ship 50,000 Shrike Strike Drones to Ukraine in $100M Deal
shortDescription: Auterion and Ukrainian drone-maker Skyfall will deliver 50,000 Shrike FPV strike drones — each running Auterion's Skynode S for terminal guidance in GPS- and comms-denied environments — under a contract funded by a European NATO nation, worth about $100M to Auterion.
whyItMatters: A concrete example of software-defined autonomy scaling into mass-manufactured munitions, funded through a NATO ally. Auterion's swarming-via-software-update model is exactly the kind of dual-use, iterate-fast play SVDG members track.

title: Skapion Emerges From Stealth With $36M to Build 'Iron Dome for Drone Swarms'
shortDescription: Washington, DC-based Skapion, founded by former Iron Dome and David's Sling leaders, launched with $36M in seed funding co-led by UP Partners and Khosla Ventures to build a mobile counter-swarm system aimed at intercepting hundreds of drones at once at far lower per-shot cost.
whyItMatters: Counter-UAS economics are the central problem in air defense right now, and a team with Rafael/Iron Dome pedigree plus Khosla money is a credible bet. One to watch on the NatSec100 radar as swarm defense heats up.

title: Dassault and Harmattan AI Fly NAMIB Electronic-Warfare Payload on Collaborative UAV With Rafale
shortDescription: Dassault Aviation and startup Harmattan AI completed a collaborative in-flight demo pairing a Rafale F4 with a drone carrying NAMIB, a new EW payload that detected and geolocated a radar dozens of kilometers away and cued a simulated strike — developed in just six months.
whyItMatters: A fast-moving prime-plus-startup partnership pushing manned-unmanned teaming and autonomous EW into a frontline fighter. Signals how European autonomy work is compressing timelines the way the US new-guard has.

title: Pentagon Awards AeroVironment $500M Contract for Counter-Drone Technology
shortDescription: The Army awarded AeroVironment a three-year, up-to-$500M IDIQ to rapidly field commercial counter-UAS gear — sensors, jammers, lasers, and interceptors — to protect troops and bases, running through June 2029.
whyItMatters: Counter-drone is the acquisition priority everyone's chasing after the Iran war exposed the 'cost curve' problem, and a $500M commercial-first IDIQ is exactly the fast-fielding, nontraditional-friendly vehicle SVDG cares about. Watch it as a template for how the Army buys attritable defense at speed.

title: Singularity Emerges From Stealth With $80M Series A at $400M Valuation for Low-Cost Air Defense
shortDescription: Singularity came out of two years in stealth with an $80M Series A led by Khosla Ventures and Felicis at a $400M valuation, building a clean-sheet, missile-based air defense interceptor aimed at breaking the cost curve on shooting down drones, Shaheds, and glide bombs. The company is flight-testing multiple times a month and building all subsystems, including the rocket motor, in-house.
whyItMatters: Low-cost air defense is one of the hottest corners of defense tech right now, and a $400M valuation out of stealth signals investors still see huge upside. The Ukraine-forged, vertically integrated, automotive-style production pitch is exactly the dual-use profile SVDG tracks.

title: Valarian Raises $50M Series A Led by NEA to Build Sovereign Infrastructure for Defense and AI
shortDescription: UK-based Valarian raised a $50M Series A led by New Enterprise Associates to scale its ACRA sovereign-infrastructure control layer, which ring-fences data and compute in mission-specific compartments across cloud, on-prem, and edge. The round marks NEA's first defense/dual-use investment in Europe.
whyItMatters: A top US venture firm making its first European defense bet is a notable signal for transatlantic capital formation, and data sovereignty is becoming a procurement gating issue for allied governments. Valarian's fast shift from B2G to B2B2G revenue is a useful template for dual-use scaling.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges for Hypersonics and Energetics
shortDescription: Spinifex Space launched to offer private test-and-evaluation range access from more than 3M acres in southwestern Queensland, spun out of solid-rocket-motor maker Black Sky Industries. Its Black Sands Test Range and Outback Space Port cover static fire, hypersonic vehicles, kinetic effectors, and energetics testing as an alternative to the government-owned Woomera range.
whyItMatters: As Australia shifts from buying missiles to building them under AUKUS, testing capacity becomes the bottleneck, and privately held range infrastructure is a scarce, strategically important asset. This is a concrete sovereign-capability play worth watching for the Australia workstream.

title: SDA Awards $1.75B to L3Harris and Sierra Space for 36 Golden Dome Missile-Tracking Satellites
shortDescription: The Space Development Agency issued $1.75B in fixed-price OTA prototype awards — $955M to L3Harris and $798M to Sierra Space — to build 36 Tranche 3 tracking-layer satellites supporting the Golden Dome missile-defense initiative, with delivery targeted by the end of 2028. Sierra Space's 18 satellites use its Horizon/Eclipse platform from the Tranche 2 program.
whyItMatters: Golden Dome is turning into one of the largest new sources of space and missile-defense demand, and the OTA prototyping route keeps signaling how DoD wants to move fast on constellations. Useful context for members tracking the space-defense industrial base and acquisition pathways.

title: Pentagon Announces Immediate Suspension of CMMC Phase II Cybersecurity Mandates
shortDescription: The Pentagon immediately suspended the CMMC Phase II third-party cybersecurity assessment mandates that were set to take effect November 10, 2026, and launched a 60-day reform review, citing costs and a severe assessor shortfall — roughly 100 assessors for over 100,000 businesses needing certification. Phase I self-assessment requirements remain in force.
whyItMatters: CMMC compliance has been one of the biggest barriers keeping small and nontraditional firms out of the defense market, so a pause plus reform review is directly relevant to SVDG's acquisition-reform and industrial-base priorities. Expect strong reactions on both sides from industry.

title: Saronic Picks Brownsville for $3B 'Port Alpha' Autonomous Shipyard
shortDescription: Autonomous vessel-maker Saronic will invest more than $3B in Port Alpha, a software-defined shipyard at the Port of Brownsville, Texas, on an 835-acre site with room to expand to ~4,400 acres. Construction starts this year with an opening targeted for 2028.
whyItMatters: This is defense-industrial-base capacity being built by a venture-backed newcomer rather than a legacy prime — exactly the shift SVDG tracks. A ~10,000-job, robotics-heavy yard aimed at mass-producing autonomous vessels is a real bet that software-defined shipbuilding can outpace the traditional model.

title: Machina Labs Wins Lockheed Martin Qualification Contract for JASSM Missile Parts
shortDescription: SVDG sponsor Machina Labs signed a qualification contract with Lockheed Martin to deliver AI-formed metal structures (fuel tanks) for the JASSM cruise missile. The work will run through Machina's forthcoming 200,000-sq-ft Factory 3, with production potentially starting next year.
whyItMatters: A sponsor moving from pilot to qualified supplier on a flagship munitions program is a concrete proof point that AI-driven flexible manufacturing can plug into prime supply chains. Bonus: Lockheed Martin Ventures is also in the SVDG orbit, so this is two portfolio names in one deal.

title: Ursa Major Wins $10M Navy Contract for MK 104 Standard Missile Rocket Motors
shortDescription: The U.S. Navy awarded Ursa Major a $10M development contract to advance its MK 104 solid rocket motor design through critical design review and static fire. The MK 104 powers the Navy's Standard Missile family (SM-2/-3/-6), and Ursa Major will build via its adaptable 'Lynx' SRM production line.
whyItMatters: Solid rocket motors are a chronic industrial-base bottleneck, and here a nontraditional propulsion startup is being funded to break into a legacy-dominated line. It's a small dollar figure but a meaningful signal on second-sourcing critical munitions.

title: Pentagon Signs Framework Deals for Thousands of Affordable Air-Launched Cruise Missiles
shortDescription: The Air Force reached framework agreements with Anduril (Barracuda-500), CoAspire, and Zone 5 (Rusty Dagger) under its Family of Affordable Mass Missiles (FAMM) program, targeting cruise missiles near $218K per round to enable mass buys of thousands over five years.
whyItMatters: Affordable mass and second-sourcing munitions from nontraditional vendors is the acquisition-reform thesis in action. Framework agreements that let the DoD buy at volume from newcomers like Anduril and Zone 5 are exactly the market-structure shift SVDG members are positioned around.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C to Scale Multi-Orbit Satellite Production
shortDescription: Swiss satellite maker SWISSto12 closed a $70M Series C on the back of $500M+ in contracts and $140M in 2025 revenue. Its 3D-printed HummingSat targets smaller, cheaper GEO satellites; funds will scale manufacturing for commercial and sovereign government customers.
whyItMatters: A profitable European space-hardware startup raising to meet sovereign-government demand is a useful read on where allied dual-use capital is flowing. The additive-manufacturing angle and multi-orbit push make it relevant to both space and industrial-base watchers.

title: Pentagon Invests $25M in Rare Earths Startup ReElement Technologies
shortDescription: The DoD will invest $25M in ReElement Technologies to build a chromatography-based rare-earth and critical-minerals refining facility in Marion, Indiana, recycling end-of-life magnets and producing germanium and gallium. It follows the company dropping an earlier $80M Pentagon loan bid.
whyItMatters: Critical-minerals independence from China is a durable SVDG theme, and this is the government taking a direct equity-style stake to stand up domestic refining. The pivot from a stalled loan to a direct investment is also a telling data point on how DoD is deploying capital into the industrial base.

title: Neros Awarded $500M Army IDIQ for Archer Attritable Drones
author: Kristen Smith
shortDescription: The Army awarded drone startup Neros a five-year, firm-fixed-price IDIQ worth up to $500M for its Archer FPV attritable drones under the Purpose-Built Attritable Systems (PBAS) program, covering the platform plus support equipment, testing, and training. Neros is producing over 1,000 drones a week and building toward a million a year by 2028.
whyItMatters: One of the largest attritable-drone commitments yet to a nontraditional, VC-backed manufacturer building China-free hardware at $2K a unit — a template for how the Army plans to buy cheap mass drones. Worth watching how much of that $500M ceiling actually converts to orders.

title: Lockheed Martin Ventures Opens London Office, Earmarks $100M+ for UK & European Defense Startups
shortDescription: Lockheed Martin Ventures is opening a London office and committing at least $100M of its recently expanded $1B fund to UK and European defense startups and allied supply chains, aiming to invest earlier in the startup lifecycle and strengthen the transatlantic industrial base.
whyItMatters: An SVDG sponsor doubling down on transatlantic defense-tech capital formation — a clear signal that US primes are now racing European VCs (Lakestar, the NATO Innovation Fund) to back allied startups. Directly relevant to sponsor engagement and the capital-formation thesis.

title: Office of Strategic Capital Launches National Security Fund Finance Program for Critical Minerals
shortDescription: OSC introduced the National Security Fund Finance (NSFF) program, a fund-level financing initiative providing direct loans to qualified investment-fund managers who pair OSC capital with private money to invest in companies addressing critical-minerals shortages, gaps, and vulnerabilities. A formal Notice of Funding Opportunity is imminent.
whyItMatters: A new OSC fund-of-funds mechanism is exactly the capital-formation tooling SVDG tracks — the government de-risking private credit funds to shore up critical-minerals supply chains. Members raising or deploying capital in this space should watch the coming NOFO.

title: Lakestar Closes Oversubscribed €262M ($300M) Resilience I Fund for European Defense & Dual-Use
shortDescription: European VC Lakestar closed an oversubscribed €262M ($300M) Resilience I fund dedicated to defense and dual-use companies across Europe and NATO-allied countries, raised entirely from private capital. Its advisory board includes Mike Pompeo and Gen. Sir Nicholas Carter.
whyItMatters: Billed as Europe's largest institutional venture fund built purely for dual-use/defense — more evidence the continent is standing up serious sovereign defense-tech capital (Lakestar backed Helsing, Isar, ARX). A useful data point for the capital-formation and international workstreams.

title: Greenjets Raises $40M Series A Backed by NATO Innovation Fund for Low-Cost Interceptors
shortDescription: UK aerospace startup Greenjets raised a $40M (€35M) Series A led by Blossom Capital with the NATO Innovation Fund and UK NSSIF, days after winning a UK MoD LCADE/LEAP contract to develop low-cost drone interceptors. Funding will scale production ~8x and expand into the US, Middle East, and India.
whyItMatters: A dual-use electric-propulsion play riding the UK/European push for affordable counter-drone interceptors — and another NATO Innovation Fund bet. Relevant to the international workstream and the low-cost air defense theme SVDG has been tracking.

title: DIU Collapses Seven Tech Portfolios Into Three as Owen West Targets 'Cost Per Kill'
author: Brandi Vincent
shortDescription: In an exclusive interview, DIU director Owen West laid out a reorganization that collapses the unit's seven technology portfolios into three — drones and autonomous warfare, kill webs (commercial space, networks, sensors, C2), and '10x' technologies — with AI treated as a horizontal across all three. Now a permanent DoD Field Activity with a roughly $1B budget, DIU will publish public scorecards tracking speed-to-contract, fielded capabilities, and cost reduction, with a 'part two' of metrics due later this summer.
whyItMatters: This is the clearest map yet of where DIU money and attention will actually go, and it's a direct signal to anyone selling into the unit: if your pitch doesn't land in drones/autonomy, kill webs, or a genuine 10x cost-or-speed shift, you're now outside the lanes. West explicitly cites the ~$250B of venture capital flowing into defense tech as a reason the reorg matters — worth reading closely before your next DIU conversation.

title: UK Defence Innovation Sets Five Core Themes to Steer £400M+ Annual Innovation Budget
shortDescription: UK Defence Innovation, the MOD's consolidated innovation team inside the National Armaments Director Group, announced five core themes that will guide its ringfenced annual budget of at least £400M: Autonomy, Decision Advantage, Logistics and Support, Effects, and Protection. National Armaments Director Rupert Pearce framed the themes as the framework for working with start-ups and scale-ups on home-grown dual-use technology.
whyItMatters: The UK has been consolidating a famously fragmented innovation estate into one front door, and this is the taxonomy US and allied companies will be pitched against. If you have a portfolio company eyeing UK entry — or an SVDG member tracking allied capital formation — these five buckets are now the vocabulary for UKDI's live funding calls.

title: DIU and Navy Open $5M Specular MIST Prize Challenge for Containerized USV Payloads
shortDescription: DIU, the Navy's Rapid Capabilities Office, and Naval Information Warfare Center Pacific launched Specular MIST, a multiphase prize challenge with a $5M pool for containerized payloads on manned and unmanned surface vessels. Lines of effort cover threat radar simulators, active electronic attack, and passive electronic surveillance, with phase-two awards up to $250K, an at-sea demo in Southern California in September, and up to $1M plus a follow-on prototype or production contract for finalists.
whyItMatters: A rare open door into maritime EW with a real production path at the end of it — and submissions close July 22, so this is time-sensitive for any member company working on USV payloads or open-architecture EW. Also a useful data point on how DIU is running the new 'kill webs' portfolio in practice.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed and Opens Brooklyn Factory for Deployable Space Solar Arrays
shortDescription: Y Combinator W26 alum Beyond Reach Labs closed a $10M seed led by Interlagos, with TerraForge Capital, Off-Piste Capital, Y Combinator, and Augur VC participating. The company's patented deployer flat-packs into a standard launch fairing and extends up to 100m, and it says it has signed $342.6M in customer LOIs since demoing in March. A new 16,000 sq ft Brooklyn facility targets 10+ units per day by end of 2027.
whyItMatters: Power is quietly becoming the binding constraint on everything the space community wants to build next — in-orbit data centers, large ISR constellations, commercial stations — and this is a bet on selling the picks and shovels rather than the platform. The LOI-to-funding ratio here is eye-catching enough to be worth a skeptical second look.

title: Anduril and Archer Unveil 'Thunder' Autonomous Tiltrotor Attack Aircraft at Farnborough
shortDescription: Anduril used day one of the Farnborough International Airshow to reveal Thunder, a Group 5 hybrid-electric tiltrotor attack drone built with Archer Aviation and UK partners including GKN Aerospace. It carries an Apache-class payload — up to 10 air-to-ground missiles, 16 launched effects, or 76 rockets, plus 12 cUAS interceptors in the nose — with a range in the thousands of nautical miles and first flight targeted for 2027.
whyItMatters: This is Anduril building a UK-based platform with UK suppliers and unveiling it on UK soil — a template for how US defense tech scales into allied markets rather than just exporting into them. It also lands squarely adjacent to the British Army's Project Nyx Apache wingman competition (Anduril is one of four downselects, winners this fall), so watch whether Thunder becomes the commercial answer to a program it technically isn't bidding.

title: X-Bow Launches Buckler, a Flight-Tested Sub-$100K Supersonic Drone Interceptor
shortDescription: New Mexico solid-rocket-motor startup X-Bow Systems unveiled Buckler at Farnborough — an already-flight-tested interceptor built to kill Shahed-class Group 3 drones at supersonic speed for under $100,000 a round. X-Bow builds the airframe, solid rocket motor, and propellant in-house, and says the Pentagon has already requested a briefing.
whyItMatters: SVDG sponsor shipping directly into the sharpest demand signal in the business: the US burned through roughly 300 THAAD and 1,430 Patriot interceptors in Iran against FY26 deliveries of 92 and 172. Vertical integration on propulsion is the interesting part — that's the exact industrial-base bottleneck everyone else is stuck behind, and it's why Lockheed led X-Bow's $105M Series B last year.

title: Shield AI and GE Aerospace Complete Thrust-Vectoring Nozzle Engine Test for X-BAT
shortDescription: Shield AI and GE Aerospace finished integration, actuation, and engine light-off testing of the AVEN thrust-vectoring nozzle mated to an F110-GE-129E engine at GE's Peebles, Ohio site — the propulsion milestone gating vertical flight for Shield AI's X-BAT autonomous VTOL fighter. AVEN is 1990s F-16 heritage hardware adapted for the fast gimbaling that vertical flight demands.
whyItMatters: X-BAT is the boldest bet in the mini-fighter category — roughly $27M a copy against an F-35's $83-110M, no runway required — and propulsion was always the make-or-break. Reusing flight-proven 1990s hardware instead of starting from zero is a nice case study in how venture-backed primes actually compress timelines, versus the clean-sheet instinct.

title: Space Force Triples NSSL Phase 3 Lane 1 Ceiling by $11.4B to $17 Billion
author: Frank Wolfe
shortDescription: Space Systems Command raised the ceiling on National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contracts from $5.6B to $17B, with future task orders flowing to the seven onramped providers: ULA, SpaceX, Blue Origin, Stoke Space, Rocket Lab, Impulse Space, and Relativity Federal.
whyItMatters: A tripled ceiling is the government telling four venture-backed launch companies that the revenue runway is real, not a pilot — the clearest capital-formation signal in space this month. It also underlines how completely DoD's space posture, from Golden Dome to PWSA, now rides on commercial providers.

title: Lockheed Unveils PAC-3 ACE, a Low-Cost Patriot Interceptor, and MORFIUS Drone-Zapper at Farnborough
shortDescription: At Farnborough, Lockheed Martin introduced the PAC-3 ACE, an interceptor priced around $1.5M-$2M (roughly a third of a PAC-3 MSE) that covers about two-thirds of the MSE's defended area, plus MORFIUS X-Rotor, a reusable airborne high-power microwave drone-killer Lockheed says can neutralize 50+ drones per flight. Lockheed plans to base ACE production in Europe with European suppliers.
whyItMatters: This is a prime moving on cost-per-kill on its own initiative — the exact critique the defense innovation crowd has been leveling for years. The European production plan is also a tell about where the demand signal (and the industrial policy pressure) actually is right now.

title: Capitol Meridian Partners Raises $1.9B for Second Fund, Hits Hard Cap
author: Win Warfield
shortDescription: Washington, D.C.-based Capitol Meridian Partners closed a $1.9B second fund at its hard cap, 50% above target, anchored by $1.8B from 60+ institutional LPs with ~90% of Fund I investors re-upping. The firm, founded by ex-Carlyle partners in 2021, now manages over $3B focused on government, aerospace and defense.
whyItMatters: Doubling Fund I in a quarter of the time is a clean read on how deep institutional appetite for defense-focused buyout capital has gotten. Worth watching as a bid for the mid-market NatSec companies that sit just past the venture stage.

title: Trump Executive Order Tightens Waiver Rules for Defense Contractors Sourcing Critical Minerals from China
shortDescription: A new executive order requires defense contractors seeking waivers to buy critical minerals and materials from China and other prohibited suppliers to prove they searched for alternatives, disclose material origins, and submit a de-risking plan. Waivers stop being issued entirely as of Jan. 1, 2027, and the Pentagon must write rules requiring contractors to map supply chains from raw material to finished system.
whyItMatters: The Jan. 2027 hard stop turns supply chain mapping from a compliance nicety into a contract-eligibility requirement — which is both a real burden for existing suppliers and an obvious opening for domestic critical-minerals and traceability startups.

title: UK Space Agency Announces £62M ($83M) for Domestic Satcom and Space Tech at Farnborough
shortDescription: The UK committed £62M ($83.3M) in new funding announced at Farnborough: £42M through the third C-LEO round for satcom supply-chain chokepoints (on-board processing, active antennas, optical links, networking, user terminals) and a new £20M National Space Innovation Programme call, with up to 40% aimed at space domain awareness and in-space servicing. A £37M Space Clusters Infrastructure Fund call follows this fall.
whyItMatters: Explicitly framed as buying down dependency on foreign space capability rather than as science funding — the same sovereignty logic driving EU and Australian programs. Relevant for any SVDG member company with a UK entity or allied-market ambitions.
